denial_notice_text.statement:
Denial reason text: 'Request denied. Member has a prior authorization denial history for cervical spine surgery (three-level fusion denied May 2024). Review of current request in context of prior denial pattern suggests the member's cervical pathology has not materially changed since the prior denial. Current request does not demonstrate sufficient change in clinical status to warrant reconsideration.' Reviewer: Dr. Alan Foster, MD. Reason code: MED-003.

The denial cites 'prior PA history' and 'prior denial pattern' as the basis. The denial does NOT cite any specific plan criterion under § 2.2 (conservative treatment), § 2.3 (imaging), § 2.1 (diagnosis), or § 2.4 (documentation). The CC-SPINE-2024 policy does NOT authorize 'prior PA denial history' as a criterion for denial; each request is evaluated against current criteria. The denial rationale is procedurally questionable on this ground.

primary_diagnosis.statement: Cervical radiculopathy C5-C6, progressive, persistent after conservative treatment.

imaging_findings.statement: Current MRI (2024-10-15): C5-C6 large disc herniation with moderate-severe right C6 nerve root compression — interval INCREASE in size from prior MRI. C4-C5: minimal change. C6-C7: minimal change. New foraminal stenosis at C5-C6.
Prior MRI (2024-04-20, at time of prior denial): C5-C6 moderate disc herniation. C4-C5 mild changes. C6-C7 mild changes.

imaging_reports.statement: Current MRI shows large C5-C6 herniation with moderate-severe right C6 compression — interval worsening from prior MRI 6 months ago.

imaging_dates.statement: Current MRI 2024-10-15, within 6 months of appeal date 2024-12-05. Prior MRI 2024-04-20 available for comparison.

serial_imaging_comparison.statement: Serial imaging comparison: C5-C6 herniation has increased in size from moderate (April 2024) to large with moderate-severe compression (October 2024). New foraminal stenosis at C5-C6. Other levels (C4-C5, C6-C7) show minimal change. Documented interval progression of pathology at C5-C6.

neurological_exam_findings.statement: Right C6 dermatomal numbness. Bicep reflex ABSENT right. Bicep weakness 3+/5 right — interval worsening from prior exam (previously 4+/5). Pain 8/10 at rest. Patient unable to continue working full-time.

neurological_exam_notes.statement: Right C6 numbness, absent bicep reflex, 3+/5 bicep weakness, severe pain. Interval worsening from prior exam.

functional_status.statement: Functional impairment: unable to continue working full-time due to symptoms. Motor deficit (3+/5 weakness, absent reflex). Pain 8/10 at rest. Radiculopathy presentation; no mJOA documented.

functional_outcome_measures.statement: PT records document the 8-week course (July-September 2024) with functional plateau explicitly documented: 'no further improvement expected, neurological deficit persistent and worsening.' This is formal PT documentation matching the clinical-standard plateau definition.

symptoms_documented.statement: C5-C6 right-sided radiculopathy with motor and reflex deficits. Strong symptom-imaging correlation: C5-C6 large herniation with right C6 nerve compression matches right C6 dermatomal symptoms and bicep findings. Documented interval worsening on both imaging and exam.

proposed_surgical_level.statement: ACDF C5-C6, single level (CPT 22551). Matches imaging level (C5-C6 herniation) and symptom level (right C6 distribution). Note: this is SINGLE-LEVEL surgery, distinct from the THREE-LEVEL fusion (C4-C5, C5-C6, C6-C7) that was the subject of the prior PA denial in May 2024.

clinical_guidelines_applicability.statement: C5-C6 radiculopathy with motor deficit (3+/5 weakness, absent bicep reflex), strong imaging-symptom correlation, 8 weeks PT with plateau documented, two pharmacotherapy agents, completed injection, progressive symptoms with interval worsening on serial imaging and exam. Per AANS/CNS and NASS, this presentation supports surgery (Tier 2 reasonable indication, with progressive deficit potentially supporting Tier 1 indication).

pt_records.statement: 8-week supervised PT course (July-September 2024), 24 sessions. This is a NEW course of PT specific to the current request, separate from any earlier PT. Functional plateau explicitly documented: 'no further improvement expected, neurological deficit persistent and worsening.'

pt_discharge_summary.statement: PT discharge summary documents functional plateau: 'no further improvement expected, neurological deficit persistent and worsening.' This is formal therapist documentation matching the clinical-standard plateau definition.

pt_progress_notes.statement: PT progress notes throughout the 8-week course document persistent and worsening neurological deficit despite therapy.

pharmacotherapy_records.statement: Two agent classes documented: (1) Gabapentin 600mg TID (neuropathic agent) for 6 months — partial pain relief, deficit persists; (2) Methylprednisolone dose pack x2 (oral corticosteroid) — temporary relief only. Both agents trialed; substrate should evaluate whether 'dose pack x2' satisfies § 2.2(b) minimum 4 weeks duration for the corticosteroid (typical dose pack is 6 days).

interventional_records.statement: Right C6 epidural steroid injection performed 2024-11-05: 50% relief for 2 weeks, returned to baseline. § 2.2(c) interventional requirement met by completed epidural.

conservative_treatment_history.statement: Conservative treatment summary for current request: 8 weeks PT with explicit functional plateau documentation; two pharmacotherapy agents (gabapentin for 6 months, methylprednisolone dose pack x2); one interventional procedure (epidural). Note: this is a NEW course of conservative treatment specific to the current single-level request, distinct from any treatment that preceded the prior three-level denial.

treating_physician_documentation.statement: Dr. Victor Osei (Spine Surgery, UCSF) appeal letter addresses the denial basis directly: 'The prior denial was for a three-level fusion (C4-C5, C5-C6, C6-C7) — a different and more extensive procedure. The current request is for single-level C5-C6 only. The prior reviewer specifically noted that single-level C5-C6 would have been approved at that time. Clinical status has worsened since May 2024: new MRI shows interval increase in C5-C6 pathology, new exam shows interval worsening of motor function, new 8-week PT course with documented plateau, new injection completed.'

Appeal basis: 'Prior denial was for three-level fusion (different procedure) — current request is single-level. Clinical status has materially changed since May 2024 — new MRI, new conservative treatment course. Current request meets all criteria independently of prior PA history. Using prior PA history as a denial basis is not authorized under CC-SPINE-2024.'

Prior PA denial (2024-05-15) was for three-level ACDF C4-C5, C5-C6, C6-C7. The prior denial reason stated: 'Three-level fusion not supported. C4-C5 and C6-C7 had insufficient imaging findings. Only C5-C6 met criteria. Two-level and three-level requests denied; single-level C5-C6 would have been approved at that time.' The prior reviewer's note that single-level C5-C6 would have been approved is highly relevant to evaluating whether the current single-level request meets criteria.
